#### R/methods.R — normalizationFactors(calls), sizeFactors(calls), counts.DESeqDataSet(function), counts(method), design(method), +10 more

```r
1	counts.DESeqDataSet <- function(object, normalized=FALSE, replaced=FALSE) {
2	  # Temporary hack for backward compatibility with "old" DESeqDataSet
3	  # objects. Remove once all serialized DESeqDataSet objects around have
4	  # been updated.
5	  if (!.hasSlot(object, "rowRanges"))
6	    object <- updateObject(object)
7	  if (replaced) {
8	    if ("replaceCounts" %in% assayNames(object)) {
9	      cnts <- assays(object)[["replaceCounts"]]
10	    } else {
11	      warning("there are no assays named 'replaceCounts', using original.
12	calling DESeq() will replace outliers if they are detected and store this assay.")
13	      cnts <- assays(object)[["counts"]]
14	    }
15	  } else {
16	    cnts <- assays(object)[["counts"]]
17	  }
18	  if (!normalized) {
19	    return(cnts)
20	  } else {
21	    if (!is.null(normalizationFactors(object))) {
22	      return( cnts / normalizationFactors(object) )
23	    } else if (is.null(sizeFactors(object)) || any(is.na(sizeFactors(object)))) {
24	      stop("first calculate size factors, add normalizationFactors, or set normalized=FALSE")
25	    } else {
26	      return( t( t( cnts ) / sizeFactors(object) ) )
27	    }
28	  }
29	}

```r
1	#' @rdname DESeqDataSet
2	#' @export
3	setClass("DESeqDataSet",
4	         contains = "RangedSummarizedExperiment",
5	         representation = representation( 
6	           design = "ANY",
7	           dispersionFunction = "function"))
8	
9	setValidity("DESeqDataSet", function(object) {
10	  if (! ("counts" %in% assayNames(object)) )

... (gap) ...

488	}
489	
490	# unexported helper function
491	processTximeta <- function(se) {
492	  assay(se) <- round(assay(se))
493	  mode(assay(se)) <- "integer"
494	  stopifnot("countsFromAbundance" %in% names(metadata(se)))
495	  cfa <- metadata(se)$countsFromAbundance
496	  stopifnot(cfa %in% c("no","scaledTPM","lengthScaledTPM"))
497	  lengthPresent <- "length" %in% assayNames(se)
498	  if (cfa %in% c("scaledTPM","lengthScaledTPM")) {
499	    message("using just counts from tximeta")
500	  } else {
501	    if (lengthPresent) {
502	      message("using counts and average transcript lengths from tximeta")
503	      stopifnot(all(assays(se)$length > 0))
504	      anms <- assayNames(se)
505	      assayNames(se)[anms == "length"] <- "avgTxLength"
506	    } else {
507	      message("length assay is missing, using original counts without length adjustment")
508	    }
509	  }
510	  return(se)
511	}
512	
```

2353	calculateCooksDistance <- function(object, H, modelMatrix) {
2354	  p <- ncol(modelMatrix)
2355	  dispersions <- robustMethodOfMomentsDisp(object, modelMatrix)
2356	  V <- assays(object)[["mu"]] + dispersions * assays(object)[["mu"]]^2
2357	  PearsonResSq <- (counts(object) - assays(object)[["mu"]])^2 / V
2358	  cooks <- PearsonResSq / p  * H / (1 - H)^2
2359	  cooks
2360	}

... (gap) ...

2366	# Cook's distance is considered for those samples with 3 or more replicates per cell
2367	#
2368	# if m == p or there are no samples over which to calculate max Cook's, then give NA
2369	recordMaxCooks <- function(design, colData, modelMatrix, cooks, numRow) {
2370	    samplesForCooks <- nOrMoreInCell(modelMatrix, n=3)
2371	    p <- ncol(modelMatrix)
2372	    m <- nrow(modelMatrix)
2373	    maxCooks <- if ((m > p) & any(samplesForCooks)) {
2374	      apply(cooks[,samplesForCooks,drop=FALSE], 1, max)
2375	    } else {
2376	      rep(NA, numRow)
2377	    }
2378	    maxCooks
2379	}
2380	
2381	# for each sample in the model matrix,
2382	# are there n or more replicates in the same cell
2383	# (including that sample)
2384	# so for a 2 x 3 comparison, the returned vector for n = 3 is:
2385	# FALSE, FALSE, TRUE, TRUE, TRUE
2386	nOrMoreInCell <- function(modelMatrix, n){
2387	  row_hash <- apply(modelMatrix, 1, paste0, collapse = "_")
2388	  hash_table <- table(row_hash)
2389	  numEqual <- as.vector(unname(hash_table[row_hash]))
2390	  numEqual >= n
2391	}
2392	
2393	
2394	# an unexported diagnostic function
2395	# to retrieve the covariance matrix
2396	# for the GLM coefficients of a single row
2397	# only for standard model matrices
2398	covarianceMatrix <- function(object, rowNumber) {
2399	  if (attr(object, "modelMatrixType") != "standard")
2400	    stop("only for standard model matrices")
2401	  # convert coefficients to log scale
2402	  coefColumns <- names(mcols(object))[grep("log2 fold change",mcols(mcols(object))$description)]
2403	  beta <- log(2) * as.numeric(as.data.frame(mcols(object)[rowNumber,coefColumns,drop=FALSE]))
2404	  x <- getModelMatrix(object)
2405	  y <- counts(object)[rowNumber,]
2406	  sf <- sizeFactors(object)
2407	  alpha <- dispersions(object)[rowNumber]
2408	  mu.hat <- as.vector(sf * exp(x %*% beta))
2409	  minmu <- 0.5
2410	  mu.hat[mu.hat < minmu] <- minmu
2411	  w <- diag(1/(1/mu.hat^2 * ( mu.hat + alpha * mu.hat^2 )))
2412	  betaPriorVar <- attr(object,"betaPriorVar")
2413	  ridge <- diag(1/(log(2)^2 * betaPriorVar))
2414	  sigma <- solve(t(x) %*% w %*% x + ridge) %*% (t(x) %*% w %*% x) %*% t(solve(t(x) %*% w %*% x + ridge))
2415	  # convert back to log2 scale
2416	  sigmaLog2Scale <- log2(exp(1))^2 * sigma
2417	  sigmaLog2Scale
2418	}
2419

2459	momentsDispEstimate <- function(object) {

2461	    mean(1/MatrixGenerics::colMeans(normalizationFactors(object)))
2462	  } else {
2463	    mean(1/sizeFactors(object))
2464	  }
2465	  bv <- mcols(object)$baseVar
2466	  bm <- mcols(object)$baseMean
2467	  (bv - xim*bm)/bm^2
2468	}

1	counts.DESeqDataSet <- function(object, normalized=FALSE, replaced=FALSE) {
2	  # Temporary hack for backward compatibility with "old" DESeqDataSet
3	  # objects. Remove once all serialized DESeqDataSet objects around have
4	  # been updated.
5	  if (!.hasSlot(object, "rowRanges"))
6	    object <- updateObject(object)
7	  if (replaced) {
8	    if ("replaceCounts" %in% assayNames(object)) {
9	      cnts <- assays(object)[["replaceCounts"]]
10	    } else {
11	      warning("there are no assays named 'replaceCounts', using original.
12	calling DESeq() will replace outliers if they are detected and store this assay.")
13	      cnts <- assays(object)[["counts"]]
14	    }
15	  } else {
16	    cnts <- assays(object)[["counts"]]
17	  }
18	  if (!normalized) {
19	    return(cnts)
20	  } else {
21	    if (!is.null(normalizationFactors(object))) {
22	      return( cnts / normalizationFactors(object) )
23	    } else if (is.null(sizeFactors(object)) || any(is.na(sizeFactors(object)))) {
24	      stop("first calculate size factors, add normalizationFactors, or set normalized=FALSE")
25	    } else {
26	      return( t( t( cnts ) / sizeFactors(object) ) )
27	    }
28	  }
29	}

491	processTximeta <- function(se) {
492	  assay(se) <- round(assay(se))
493	  mode(assay(se)) <- "integer"
494	  stopifnot("countsFromAbundance" %in% names(metadata(se)))
495	  cfa <- metadata(se)$countsFromAbundance
496	  stopifnot(cfa %in% c("no","scaledTPM","lengthScaledTPM"))
497	  lengthPresent <- "length" %in% assayNames(se)
498	  if (cfa %in% c("scaledTPM","lengthScaledTPM")) {
499	    message("using just counts from tximeta")
500	  } else {
501	    if (lengthPresent) {
502	      message("using counts and average transcript lengths from tximeta")
503	      stopifnot(all(assays(se)$length > 0))
504	      anms <- assayNames(se)
505	      assayNames(se)[anms == "length"] <- "avgTxLength"
506	    } else {
507	      message("length assay is missing, using original counts without length adjustment")
508	    }
509	  }
510	  return(se)
511	}
512

2353	calculateCooksDistance <- function(object, H, modelMatrix) {
2354	  p <- ncol(modelMatrix)
2355	  dispersions <- robustMethodOfMomentsDisp(object, modelMatrix)
2356	  V <- assays(object)[["mu"]] + dispersions * assays(object)[["mu"]]^2
2357	  PearsonResSq <- (counts(object) - assays(object)[["mu"]])^2 / V
2358	  cooks <- PearsonResSq / p  * H / (1 - H)^2
2359	  cooks
2360	}

2369	recordMaxCooks <- function(design, colData, modelMatrix, cooks, numRow) {
2370	    samplesForCooks <- nOrMoreInCell(modelMatrix, n=3)
2371	    p <- ncol(modelMatrix)
2372	    m <- nrow(modelMatrix)
2373	    maxCooks <- if ((m > p) & any(samplesForCooks)) {
2374	      apply(cooks[,samplesForCooks,drop=FALSE], 1, max)
2375	    } else {
2376	      rep(NA, numRow)
2377	    }
2378	    maxCooks
2379	}
2380	
2381	# for each sample in the model matrix,
2382	# are there n or more replicates in the same cell
2383	# (including that sample)
2384	# so for a 2 x 3 comparison, the returned vector for n = 3 is:
2385	# FALSE, FALSE, TRUE, TRUE, TRUE
2386	nOrMoreInCell <- function(modelMatrix, n){
2387	  row_hash <- apply(modelMatrix, 1, paste0, collapse = "_")
2388	  hash_table <- table(row_hash)
2389	  numEqual <- as.vector(unname(hash_table[row_hash]))
2390	  numEqual >= n
2391	}

2397	# only for standard model matrices
2398	covarianceMatrix <- function(object, rowNumber) {
2399	  if (attr(object, "modelMatrixType") != "standard")
2400	    stop("only for standard model matrices")
2401	  # convert coefficients to log scale
2402	  coefColumns <- names(mcols(object))[grep("log2 fold change",mcols(mcols(object))$description)]
2403	  beta <- log(2) * as.numeric(as.data.frame(mcols(object)[rowNumber,coefColumns,drop=FALSE]))
2404	  x <- getModelMatrix(object)
2405	  y <- counts(object)[rowNumber,]
2406	  sf <- sizeFactors(object)
2407	  alpha <- dispersions(object)[rowNumber]
2408	  mu.hat <- as.vector(sf * exp(x %*% beta))
2409	  minmu <- 0.5
2410	  mu.hat[mu.hat < minmu] <- minmu
2411	  w <- diag(1/(1/mu.hat^2 * ( mu.hat + alpha * mu.hat^2 )))
2412	  betaPriorVar <- attr(object,"betaPriorVar")
2413	  ridge <- diag(1/(log(2)^2 * betaPriorVar))
2414	  sigma <- solve(t(x) %*% w %*% x + ridge) %*% (t(x) %*% w %*% x) %*% t(solve(t(x) %*% w %*% x + ridge))
2415	  # convert back to log2 scale
2416	  sigmaLog2Scale <- log2(exp(1))^2 * sigma
2417	  sigmaLog2Scale
2418	}

信息已足够完整，可以回答了。

---

**调用链（按顺序）：**

**1. 泛型（Generic）**

`estimateDispersions` — 定义于 `R/AllGenerics.R`（通过 `BiocGenerics` 继承，DESeq2 在此注册实现）。

**2. S4 方法（DESeqDataSet 签名）**

```r
# R/methods.R:704
setMethod("estimateDispersions", signature(object="DESeqDataSet"),
          estimateDispersions.DESeqDataSet)
```

实际执行体是 `estimateDispersions.DESeqDataSet`（`R/methods.R:500`），函数签名为：

```r
estimateDispersions.DESeqDataSet <- function(object,
  fitType = c("parametric","local","mean","glmGamPoi"),
  maxit = 100, useCR = TRUE, weightThreshold = 1e-2,
  quiet = FALSE, modelMatrix = NULL, minmu = ...)
```

**3. 方法体内的第一个实质性调用**

做完参数校验和守卫检查后，第一个核心调用是（`R/methods.R:556`）：

```r
object <- estimateDispersionsGeneEst(object, maxit=maxit, useCR=useCR,
                                     weightThreshold=weightThreshold, ...)
```

即 **`estimateDispersionsGeneEst()`**，负责逐基因的离散度估计（per-gene MLE），是整个离散度估计流水线的第一步。

**总结：**

| 层级 | 名称 | 位置 |
|------|------|------|
| S4 泛型 | `estimateDispersions` | `R/AllGenerics.R` (via BiocGenerics) |
| DESeqDataSet 实现 | `estimateDispersions.DESeqDataSet` | `R/methods.R:500–705` |
| 下一个调用 | `estimateDispersionsGeneEst()` | `R/methods.R:556` |
